info.description:
Target-related Postman collection extracted from the real Laravel backend after the team-based target flow update.

**Seeded demo (default emails):** leader `sales.leader@rakez.com`, marketer `sales@rakez.com`, password `password`. Run `php artisan db:seed` and `SalesLeaderTeamProjectsLinkSeeder` is in the default pipeline so team projects are non-empty.

**Collection variable `strict_seed_assertions`:** `true` (default) asserts team projects total >= 1 and stricter checks. Set to `false` if testing against an empty DB.

Auth:
- POST /api/login
- GET /api/user
- POST /api/logout

Core target APIs:
- GET /api/sales/targets/my
- GET /api/sales/targets/by-project/{contractId}
- POST /api/sales/targets
- PATCH /api/sales/targets/{id}

Team-based source of truth for leader target setup:
- GET /api/sales/team/projects
- GET /api/sales/team/members
- GET /api/sales/projects/{contractId}/units

Current backend behavior:
- Sales leader sees only completed projects linked to the leader team by project management via contract_team.
- Sales leader can create a target only for a sales marketer in the same team.
- Target can be project-level or unit-level.
- Sales marketer sees only targets assigned to them personally.
- Leader view of /api/sales/targets/my returns project cards (item_type = project_assignment) sourced from PM team linkage, not sales_project_assignments.

Suggested order:
1. Login - Sales Leader
2. Get Team Projects
3. Get Team Members
4. Get Project Units
5. Create Target
6. Get Targets By Project - Leader
7. Login - Sales Marketer
8. Get My Targets - Marketer View
9. Update Target Status
info.name: Sales Targets API

request.description: POST /api/login via App\\Http\\Controllers\\Registration\\LoginController::login. Body requires email + password.
Same login API for a sales user account. Use this token to test target ownership and status updates.
Protected auth check endpoint using the current active token.
POST /api/logout via LoginController::logout using the current active token.

exec: pm.test("Logout responds (200 or 401 if token empty)", function () {
// Empty assignee_marketer_id makes raw JSON invalid ("marketer_id": ,) and all fields look "missing" to Laravel.

request.description: Leader helper endpoint. Returns only completed projects linked to the leader team by project management via contract_team. Use this as the source of truth for contract_id when creating targets.

exec: pm.test("At least one team project (seed: SalesLeaderTeamProjectsLinkSeeder + completed contracts)", function () {

request.description: Leader helper endpoint. Returns team members available for assignment. In the current target flow, target assignee must be a sales marketer in the same team and not a manager.
Returns units for the selected PM-linked team project. Use this only after choosing contract_id from Get Team Projects or leader targets/my.

exec: pm.test("Project units request is reachable", function () {

request.description: Leader view of targets page. Current backend returns project cards sourced from contract_team (item_type = project_assignment, assignment_id = null, assigned_by = Project Management), not SalesTarget rows.

exec: pm.test("Leader targets endpoint is reachable", function () {

request.description: Leader-side lookup. Returns only targets assigned to team marketers for a project that belongs to the leader team via project management linkage.

exec: pm.test("Leader by-project returns 200 with success", function () {

request.description: Creates a **performance target** (must_sell_units_count + assigned_target_value). Does not assign inventory. Optional explicit assigned_target_value; otherwise derived from unit prices in the project. Legacy: contract_unit_id may still be sent to derive must_sell_units_count only.

exec: pm.test("Create target returns success", function () { pm.expect(json.success).to.eql(true); });

request.description: Project-level performance target: set must_sell_units_count (no concrete unit assignment). Response includes project_location.
Performance target for N units of sales (must_sell_units_count). Legacy body may send contract_unit_ids to derive count only — backend does not attach inventory.
Marketer view of /api/sales/targets/my. Current backend returns only SalesTarget rows where marketer_id equals the current user.

exec: pm.test("Marketer my targets should be authenticated", function () {

request.description: Marketer-side lookup for one project. Current backend returns only the current marketer targets inside that project, not team-wide targets.
Updates target status for the target owner only. In the current backend, only the assigned marketer can change status.

exec: pm.test("PATCH target status (200 if owner, else 403/404)", function () {

request.description: Marks a target as completed for the target owner only. In the current backend, leader does not update target status here.

exec: pm.test("PATCH target completed (200 if owner, else 403/404)", function () {
